channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: KNUT Leadership Largely Uncontested Ahead of 2026 Elections
item.description: In Kisumu, Kenya National Union of Teachers (KNUT) Secretary General Collins Oyuu has revealed that several top union positions will face no opposition in the forthcoming national elections. Speaking ...

item.title: Two 16-year-olds charged following knife incident on City Walk
item.description: ACT Policing has charged a 16-year-old boy and will summons another over an incident involving knives on City Walk in Canberra on Saturday evening, April 4, 2026. Multiple reports of a group of young ...

item.title: City of Joburg disconnects government departments over unpaid bills exceeding R13bn
item.description: The City of Johannesburg (CoJ) has begun disconnecting electricity to government departments due to substantial unpaid bills. According to recent figures, City Power is owed nearly R754 million, while...

item.title: Saskatoon authorities investigate two deaths over Easter weekend
item.description: Saskatoon experienced a tragic Easter weekend as two separate incidents resulted in fatalities. On Friday, firefighters responded to a house fire in the 400 Block of Avenue I South. During the operati...

item.title: Uttar Pradesh Police Announces 81,000+ Vacancies for 2026 Recruitment
item.description: The Uttar Pradesh Police has officially announced recruitment for the year 2026, opening over 81,000 positions across various ranks and departments. This recruitment drive is part of the state governm...

item.title: Ukraine anticipates U.S. delegation visit to Kyiv to resume peace negotiations
item.description: Ukraine is preparing for a visit from high-level U.S. envoys, including Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, possibly accompanied by Senator Lindsey Graham, to Kyiv later this month. The visit is expected...

item.title: Four passengers die as truck overturns in Aleosan, Cotabato
item.description: Four people, including three Moro women and a three-year-old boy, died instantly after a truck carrying 33 passengers overturned on a highway in Barangay San Mateo, Aleosan, Cotabato on Saturday, Apri...

item.title: Australian SAS Deployed Early to Middle East to Support Evacuation Efforts
item.description: About 40 soldiers from Australia’s elite Special Air Service (SAS) based in Perth were sent to the Middle East roughly one week after the US launched Operation Epic Fury against Iran on 28 February. T...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates with Strikes and Missile Attacks
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has escalated sharply, with Iran responding to US-Israeli airstrikes by targeting Israel and US positions in the Middle East. Following the down...

item.title: Explaining the Role and Responsibilities of the US Attorney General
item.description: President Donald Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi on April 2, 2026, marking the shortest tenure for the position in six decades. While much media attention has focused on the firing itself, fewe...

item.title: Trump Proposes $152 Million to Reopen Alcatraz as a Modern Federal Prison
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has requested $152 million in the White House's proposed budget for the 2027 fiscal year to reopen Alcatraz as a modern federal prison. The funding would cover the fir...

item.title: Trump’s Policies and Rhetoric Against Somali Immigrants in the US
item.description: In late 2025, former US President Donald Trump intensified his attacks on Somali immigrants in Minnesota, labelling them as undesirable and claiming Somali gangs were taking over the streets. This rhe...

item.title: Makau Mutua Comments on Sifuna's Position Amid ODM Disciplinary Process
item.description: In Kenya, political analyst Makau Mutua has publicly described ODM Secretary General Edwin Sifuna as a 'dead man walking' following the party's initiation of disciplinary proceedings against him. The ...

item.title: The Role of Power and Influence in International Relations
item.description: The discipline of International Relations (IR) has historically presented itself as a neutral science capable of explaining how states interact in global politics. However, closer scrutiny reveals tha...

item.title: Federal Government Declares Good Friday and Easter Monday as Public Holidays
item.description: The Federal Government don declare say tomorrow wey be Friday and Monday, April 6, 2026, go be public holidays to celebrate this year Easter. Minister of Interior, Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, announce am on b...

item.title: MP Urges Governors to Submit Extortion Evidence to Authorities Instead of Public Allegations
item.description: Suba South MP Caroli Omondi has advised Kenyan governors who are claiming to have been extorted to take their evidence to investigative agencies rather than making public accusations. Speaking on a lo...

item.title: Four individuals arrested in connection with arson attack on Jewish charity ambulances in London
item.description: A fourth individual has been arrested in connection with the arson attack on ambulances owned by a Jewish charity in north London, the Crown Prosecution Service has confirmed. The arrest occurred on S...

item.title: William and Kate keep distance from Beatrice and Eugenie amid Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor controversy
item.description: Prince William and Princess Kate have deliberately distanced themselves from Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie due to the ongoing Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor scandal. The princesses, daughters of the dis...

item.title: Heart Disease Risk in Men Begins in Early Thirties, Study Finds
item.description: Recent research published in the Journal of American Heart Association highlights that men’s risk for heart disease begins earlier than commonly perceived. Tracking over 5,000 adults for more than 30 ...

item.title: Community Shelters Help Women Work and Protect Children While Restoring Niger Lands
item.description: For Niger wey dey hot like oven, women wey dey try earn small money dey face wahala as dem get to care for pikin dem under strong sun. The Integrated Landscape Management Project (PGIP), wey World Ban...
